idiom_alternates.name: two-phase-commit-with-contract-author
idiom_alternates.rationale: Contract-first: a contract-author bead pre-stocks the shared state (codes.py + registry.py) BEFORE the 6 per-class workers run. This is execution-safe — workers can reference ErrorCode.X because the contract-author has already defined every X. Different shape from synthesis-pipeline (merge-after) but structurally sound for this case. Empirically valid for any execution layer that supports a leading contract bead.

notes: Primary reference is synthesis-pipeline: 6 sibling implementers fan into 1 merger that extends shared state. This pairs with the orchworkers worker-layer pattern (validated empirically). Two-phase-commit-with-contract-author is accepted as a structurally-equivalent alternative (sonnet's preferred shape). Both pre-stock shared-state authority in a single bead — they just differ on whether it runs before or after the workers.
